steps.title: Track the changed protein
steps.content: The researchers replaced A. thaliana's ELF3 with a similar protein from stiff brome. At 22 degrees Celsius, flowering did not differ, but at 27 degrees Celsius, unmodified plants accelerated flowering while altered plants did not. The changed protein is therefore tied to the temperature response.

steps.title: Reject overclaims
steps.content: A overstates the result by saying temperature-sensitive flowering is unique to A. thaliana. B invents increased ELF3 production, which was not measured. D misreads the 22-degree result; both altered and unmodified plants flowered there. The answer is C.
